bullets: Asian PP sliding on weak demand and recovering supply; buyers on the sidelines.
Petrochina PP yarn cut to 1,250 CIF; Sabic shocks with high 1,500-1,600 offers.
NSRP Marubeni 114G to 36,500; Sibur PP homo down 1,000 to 36,000-36,300.
ICIS shows LDPE/LLDPE/HDPE all lower; ME inventory and US flows pressure China.
Equate HDPE 6888 finalized near 1,360 CIF; HDPE Film domestic cut to 41,000.
Lotte Titan 260GG sellers down to 50,000, some to 49,000 VND/kg.
Formosa July re-offer 30-40 lower: S65/S60/S70 at 930 CIF, 5% tax.
Coal-based SG-5 Erdos to 22,500; one import signed at 22,100 (Yen Vien).
Sinopec S1000 dropped to 24,600 but buyers bidding below 23,000.

editorial.changedNote: 18 of 50 moved down, led by HDPE/LDPE

summary: ICIS shows broad PE declines across all segments in Vietnam, worsened by ME inventory build and US shipments into China.
SEA buyers avoid imports, leaning on existing stock or cheaper domestic material.
Domestic sellers cut HDPE/LDPE/LLDPE hard to clear stock as buyers stay cautious.
paras: Per ICIS on May 22, LDPE in Vietnam moved sideways at the low end but fell another 50 USD/ton at the high end to 1,450-1,550 CFR; LLDPE down 20-50 to 1,150-1,280 CFR; HDPE Film down 10-50 to 1,250-1,350 CFR; HDPE injection down 50-80 to 1,200-1,300 CFR; HDPE blow down 50 to 1,200-1,300 CFR.

PE import talks were quiet. ME inventory accumulation and more US shipments into China added downward pressure. In SEA, many buyers stayed out, relying on stock; Indonesian domestic PE kept falling. In Vietnam, Chinese-origin PE supply stayed abundant as buyers awaited arriving shipments.
A US-Iran ceasefire MOU to reopen the Strait of Hormuz drew attention; many expect ME resin supply to surge and prices to fall further if signed.
Equate Kuwait HDPE Film 6888 could finalize near 1,360 CIF, but volumes signed were small and most buyers saw it as high.
Domestically, HDPE Film sellers cut deeply: Equate 6888 and Qamar F1 down to 41,000, off ~2,000 vs last week, some ready at 40,500 to clear early. Most HDPE yarn offered below 43,000.

LDPE kept falling: Lotte Titan 260GG to 50,000 from a prior 51,000 low, with one seller at 49,000. LLDPE under heavy suppression - Chinese oil-based 7042 at 34,500 (down 400); Qamar FD21HS finalized near 36,800 with limited trade.

summary: Asian PP broadly lower on weak demand, recovering supply, and packaging plants cutting run rates.
Chinese and Russian offers fell sharply while Sabic shocked buyers with unusually high new prices.
Vietnam pressured as Marubeni cut NSRP prices and NSRP readied a large spot sale.
paras: Per ICIS on May 22, Vietnam PP yarn/injection moved sideways at the low end but fell 30 USD/ton at the high end to 1,190-1,250 CFR. Across SEA and China, PP homo and BOPP mostly fell 10-30, with IPP Film down up to 160.

Weak demand and competitive supply kept pressuring Asian PP; buyers stayed out fearing further drops. Recent resin price spikes hurt packaging makers, some cutting run rates as they could not pass on costs.
In SEA, improving domestic supply pushed makers back to local sources; weaker currencies raised import costs. More PP offers, including returning ME and Korean supply, signaled recovering supply.
A reported US-Iran ceasefire MOU to reopen the Strait of Hormuz raised expectations of further resin price falls if signed.
Petrochina PP yarn L5E89/T30S to Vietnam dropped to 1,250 CIF, up to 80 below last week. Sabic offered PP yarn/injection at a high 1,500 CIF and copo at 1,550-1,600, surprising buyers as too high.

Domestically, Marubeni offered NSRP 114G at 36,500 (with VAT), 500 lower than early last week, with buyers eyeing 36,000. NSRP readied a 27,000-ton spot sale, pressuring supply. Sibur PP homo at 36,000-36,300, down 1,000; non-woven PP at 36,600.

summary: Asian PVC kept falling on sluggish demand; makers cut offers despite high costs.
Formosa re-offered July grades 30-40 lower (S65/S60/S70 at 930 CIF) but interest stayed weak.
Cheap Chinese coal-based imports pushed Vietnam prices to market lows.
paras: Imported PVC in Asia fell again last week, with CFR China down 30 USD/ton amid sluggish demand. Ethylene-based costs stayed high on tight naphtha/ethylene, yet makers cut offers; abundant Chinese coal-based PVC kept its edge.
Formosa earlier announced June offers: S60/S65/S70/S70R at 910 FOB Taiwan and 970 CFR to SEA including Vietnam.
On May 25, Formosa re-offered to Vietnam 30-40 lower for June-July: S65/S65D/S60/S70 and B57 at 930 CIF, S57 at 940 CIF, 5% tax. Buyers were not ready at that level.
Domestically, coal-based SG-5 Erdos offered at 22,500 (down 500); one buyer signed at 22,100 at Yen Vien (Hanoi), a market low not yet common.

Oil-based pressure grew. AGC Vina and TPC Vina offered June SP660/SG660/PM66R at 22,800-23,100 ex-VAT (~24,624-24,948 with 8% VAT); TPC Vina was back to normal capacity. Sinopec S1000 had fallen to 24,600 with few trades; buyers now bid below 23,000.
